title: Audio: graph reactivation — one loop, swap the container

cues.label: Reactivation
cues.headline: You know graphs; rebuild the speed
cues.narration: This is a strong-but-stale pattern, so the goal is recall speed: pick the right traversal and code it calmly.

cues.label: One machine
cues.headline: The container sets the behavior
cues.narration: Graph traversal is one loop; a queue gives BFS, a stack gives DFS, a priority queue gives Dijkstra.

cues.label: Why BFS is shortest
cues.headline: FIFO ⇒ shortest unweighted
cues.narration: First-in-first-out forces nondecreasing distance, so the first time BFS reaches a node is along a shortest path.

cues.label: Mark on enqueue
cues.headline: Discovered equals visited
cues.narration: Mark a node visited the instant you push it, so each node enters the frontier exactly once.

cues.label: Multi-source
cues.headline: Seed every source at distance 0
cues.narration: Multi-source BFS seeds all sources together and counts layers, turning simultaneous spread into one queue loop.

cues.label: Union Find
cues.headline: Incremental connectivity
cues.narration: Union Find answers 'same group?' incrementally with path compression and union by rank in near-constant time.

cues.label: Dijkstra
cues.headline: Expand the cheapest first
cues.narration: A priority queue always expands the cheapest node, so its first pop is final — for non-negative weights only.

cues.label: Topo sort
cues.headline: Kahn's in-degree BFS
cues.narration: Topological sort is in-degree BFS; it returns fewer nodes exactly when a cycle exists.

cues.label: Traps
cues.headline: Visited timing · container · negatives
cues.narration: The bugs are visited timing, container mismatch, Dijkstra on negatives, and bare Union Find — not the ideas.
